title: Understanding Conservation of Momentum
text: Since the ice is frictionless, the total momentum of the system (player + puck) is conserved. Initially, both are at rest, so the total initial momentum is zero. After the puck is hit, the momentum of the puck and the player must still add up to zero.

title: Calculate the Player's Velocity
text: Use the conservation of momentum to find the player's velocity. The momentum of the puck is given by its mass times its velocity. Set this equal to the momentum of the player (mass times velocity) and solve for the player's velocity.

title: Player's Velocity Calculation
text: The momentum of the puck is $$0.15\times45$$. Set this equal to the player's momentum, $$90v$$, and solve for v.

title: Time for the Puck to Reach the Goal
text: Calculate the time it takes for the puck to travel $$15.0$$ $$m$$ to the goal. Use the formula: time $$=$$ $$\frac{distance}{velocity}$$.

title: Time Calculation
text: The time it takes for the puck to reach the goal is $$\frac{15}{45}$$.

title: Distance the Player Recoils
text: Use the player's velocity and the time calculated to find the distance the player recoils. Use the formula: distance $$=$$ $$velocity time$$.

title: Distance Calculation
text: The distance the player recoils is $$0.075\times0.333$$.
